item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really enjoyed the article. Once I saw "he took my heart away, put it like that" I glanced back through that paragraph and immediately understood the inspiration, which was exciting. The interview overall gives a new tragic dimension to an already freaky and disturbing anomaly. (The opening line of the discussion thread made me laugh. "Love and other things that mess you up inside"- a great summary!)

One nitpick: "Date of Death: 28/11/2014, age 42". I've never seen (day, month, year) written with slashes before. My understanding was that those were written with dashes, and slashes indicated (month, day, year).

item.title: Well Done
item.content:encoded: Very clever. +1

item.title: Re: Appreciation
item.content:encoded: One of the advantages of a character that talks expansively and metaphorically is that it greatly increases the opportunities for misdirection!

item.title: Re: Appreciation
item.content:encoded:
I literally didn't see any of that until I read the interview a second time, just now.

There is a lot of subtext in that interview, and I am impressed. GG, psul. G effin' G.

item.title: Appreciation
item.content:encoded:
… and then that night he took my - well, he took my heart away, put it like that…

Damn, but that's a clever line! Perfectly misleads but rings clear in the memory a little later once things start coming together. Same with the line about swimming in the bay. Breadcrumbs. Clever breadcrumbs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

This rings truer. The selkie theory doesn't have as much evidence in the article, I think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My first forum thank! I feel honored to have contributed in even the smallest way. This is great. That interview, their love, it's really touching. I'm not sad for her, though, because she had that for a while.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And you did it wonderfully. Perfect mix of the two.
Well done and have yourself a +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I assume the SCP is based on some mythical creature from that region

It is, at least in part. sirpudding's guess above is essentially correct, although it wasn't a literal cloak I had in mind. That said, I am intrigued by Scorpion451's guess, and am post hoc claiming it to be part of the idea!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! That tension between the highly clinical body horror, and the very loose dialogue of the tragic love story, was one of my main aims in writing this, so I'm glad it came across.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What stands out to me is the autopsy reports. I can't say I've ever read a real one, but the way they're written feels genuine somehow.

Thanks TL333s! I'm pleased that they feel real, as I read quite a few real autopsy reports (and guidance on writing them) before preparing them.

In fact, the early drafts of the autopsy extracts felt so real (and were so long) that it made the whole thing boring. I'm pleased that wasn't the case for you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow, this was a very fun read. Equal parts horror from those autopsy reports, and tragedy from that interview. While I'm not familiar enough with folk tales from that region of the world I assume the SCP is based on some mythical creature from that region, which gives this article bonus points as it doesn't come across as the usual "what if mythical creature x was an SCP"

+1. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My guess:
– hide block


She's a Leanan sídhe- they cause those they love to be inspired, but also slowly (and involuntarily) kill them.

What her husband took was her detachment- she cares now. And that walls her off from the fae.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My read
– hide block

She's whatever it is that the selkie myth is based on, but is actually something stranger. Her husband stole the seal skin cloak (may not be a literal seal skin cloak) in order to trap her. She is from someplace really weird, though and she warps biology around her, which killed her husband and the others.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really enjoyed the combination of the clinical autopsy reports and the emotional interview. Nice work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What stands out to me is the autopsy reports. I can't say I've ever read a real one, but the way they're written feels genuine somehow.

That said, after the interview, I upvoted tentatively, expecting to get an explanation of the lore behind this, but that seems to have been left out. So I'd call this a good article that I don't understand; I'm still pretty sure there's something behind it, at least.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is the good spooky. I don't even mind the killing part, as it is integral to the nature of the entity.

Does anyone else hope that it eventually gets to go home?
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Death of the author (and good humour), baby.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I still love this, still wish it had some Orkadian, and still wonder if the Nukelavee didn't slip through from the same place she came from.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, I'm afraid it's not pun based. Unless you want it to be one, as I assume Aiden might :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is this a pun based skip? Aiden Eldritch's certainly making it out to be one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: psul, it must've taken a lot of guts and a big heart to post this as you have. While I don't have as much ice or acid running through my veins, I can respect the work you've put in so far.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I remember critiquing this! This has definitely improved if I remember right : D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a story about love, and other things that can mess you up inside.

Many people to thank, as usual. To Myriad, thamuzz, sirpudding, Wogglebug, pootis spencer here, absurdemest, Liampluridon, Varren Erelim and Mf99k - I am much obliged for all your thoughts and suggestions.

Image is from wikipedia.
